title: uComDrv.dll and uComSkl.dll
text: uComDrv.dll and uComSkl.dll DLLs that provide COM support on Intel-based platforms Default location: UnifaceAPSInstallDir /common/bin Description uComDrv.dll is the COM connector DLL that implements the Uniface-COM interface. It is required if the Uniface application is performing COM call-out or call-in, and must reside in the Uniface bin directory. uComSkl.dll is a skeleton DLL file used during the creation of Uniface COM Interface DLL files. It is required if you want to generate COM Interface DLL files for Uniface services. Runtime Environment Note: The developer is responsible for including the uComDrv.dll . For COM clients to create instances of Uniface services (call-in), you must have the exported COM Interface DLL for the services on the workstation. You need to register the exported COM Interface DLL in the workstation’s Microsoft Windows Registry. This is handled by the self-registering capability of the DLL, and the standard Microsoft Windows REGSVR32 command. For example, to register the definitions contained in the file SERVS.DLL , use the following command: REGSVR32 SERVS.DLL For COM call-out, the assignment file must contain the appropriate reference to the COM connector path for the desired components. Note: COM-specific assignment file settings are not required if COM is specified as the default implementation in the Signature Editor. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
